title: Ticking Time Bombs: 3 Dividend Stocks to Dump Before the Damage Is Done
text: Undoubtedly, investors are getting nervous with ongoing concerns of higher interest rates, inflation, and a weakening economy. However, one way to improve peace of mind is selling risky dividend stocks.

title: 3 Ways to Profit from 2023's Turbulent Commercial Real Estate Market
text: This year's commercial real estate market is in a tough spot. High interest rates and remote work's newfound permanence in corporate culture combine to make commercial real estate a tough sell.

title: The REIT Meltdown Accelerates: Long-Term Investors Should Rejoice
text: REITs experienced a significant selloff after the Federal Reserve indicated that high interest rates will persist for a while. Despite the market panic, or rather because of it, dividend investors should take this opportunity to buy shares in high-quality yet attractively valued REITs. I discuss why it does not make sense to talk about how "REITs" will do this or that (despite doing it all the time myself), because the average isn't representative.

title: BXP to Release Third Quarter 2023 Financial Results on November 1, 2023
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--BXP (NYSE: BXP), the largest publicly traded developer, owner, and manager of premier workplaces in the United States, announced today that it will release financial results for the third quarter on Wednesday, November 1, 2023, after the close of trading on the NYSE. BXP will host a conference call and webcast on Thursday, November 2, 2023, at 10:00 A.M. Eastern Time, to discuss the financial results and provide an update on BXP. title: ENEL AND BXP EXECUTE POWER PURCHASE AGREEMENT FOR NEW TEXAS SOLAR PROJECT
text: Enel North America 1 will sell 21 MW of solar power from the Estonian solar project, now under construction in Delta County, Texas, to BXP. The new 202 MW2 solar project, paired with a 104 MW battery system, is expected to come online in late 2024.

title: Boston Properties - I Was Clear About The Upside
text: Quality prevails in the investment approach, willing to accept long correction periods for quality investments. REITs, including healthcare and office sectors, have been punished but some are starting to recover. Boston Properties (BXP) is an opportunity with significant upside, up over 40% since purchase.

title: Boston Properties (BXP) Crossed Above the 20-Day Moving Average: What That Means for Investors
text: Boston Properties (BXP) reached a significant support level, and could be a good pick for investors from a technical perspective. Recently, BXP broke through the 20-day moving average, which suggests a short-term bullish trend.

title: Boston Properties (BXP) Just Overtook the 50-Day Moving Average
text: After reaching an important support level, Boston Properties (BXP) could be a good stock pick from a technical perspective. BXP surpassed resistance at the 50-day moving average, suggesting a short-term bullish trend.

title: Billionaire Commercial Real Estate Investor Says 'The Worst Is Yet To Come'
text: Commercial real estate market is just beginning to experience the pain of the correction, according to billionaire investor Jeff Greene. Office buildings are expected to be the hardest hit, with declining leasing volume and potential defaults on loans. Retail, industrial, and residential real estate are expected to fare better, with little risk of oversupply and high occupancy rates.

title: Is it Wise to Retain Boston Properties (BXP) Stock for Now?
text: Healthy demand for premier office spaces and life-science portfolio expansion moves bode well for Boston Properties (BXP). However, a choppy office market scenario and high interest rates are woes.

title: Best office REIT stocks to buy as a golden cross forms
text: Office Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) companies are facing numerous challenges this year. Interest rates have jumped to a 22-year high, lowering their appeal among income investors.

title: Closing The Loop On REIT Prices And Discount Rates
text: Requiring that the value of discounted cash earnings is near the Net Asset Value explains why REITs are generally priced near NAV. It makes sense for the market to assign less value to future growth than to base cash earnings. Modeling these aspects provides some insight into which REIT sectors are opportunistic today.

title: Sell Alert: 2 REITs Getting Risky
text: I am a heavy buyer of REITs. But that doesn't mean that I am bullish on all REITs. I highlight two REITs that I sold and wouldn't buy back.

title: BXP Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Boston Properties, Inc. (NYSE: BXP), the largest publicly traded developer, owner, and manager of premier workplaces in the United States, announced today that its Board of Directors declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.98 per share of common stock for the period July 1, 2023 to September 30, 2023, payable on October 31, 2023 to shareholders of record as of the close of business on September 29, 2023. title: Boston Properties: The Stock Has Become Fairly Valued
text: Boston Properties is a high-quality office REIT with A-class properties in major cities. The company's Q2 results are pretty solid, making the company fairly valued compared to the last quarter. I present my analysis, which leads me to a hold rating.

title: Market Crash Coming? 3 Real Estate (REIT) Stocks to Buy for a Soft Landing.
text: Real estate investment trusts, or REITs, are companies that own and operate income-producing real estate in the property sector. REITs offer several benefits to investors, including high dividend payouts.
